new_value: Migrated ~60 winget packages, 7 VS Code extensions, and game launcher pinning into chezmoi dotfiles. PR #46 created.

old_value: {"id":"dotfiles-auy","title":"Review docs and populate root README.md","description":"The docs/ directory has 5 files totalling ~2900 lines: BACKUP_RECOVERY.md (577), MAINTENANCE.md (521), MIGRATION.md (649), TESTING.md (332), TROUBLESHOOTING.md (830). For a personal dotfiles repo, much of this is over-engineered. Propose a trimmed documentation set. Candidates for trimming/merging: MIGRATION.md (one-time use, could become a section in README), BACKUP_RECOVERY.md (mostly theoretical — keep age key backup essentials, drop the rest). The root README.md is currently empty and should become the primary entry point covering: what this repo is, quickstart install, what's included (tools/platforms), machine types, and links to detailed docs. Also review CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md at root level for relevance.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:45Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:45Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"dotfiles-3zp","title":"Consolidate specs into single requirements document","description":"The specs/ directory has 7 files (spec.md, plan.md, research.md, tasks.md, quickstart.md, checklists/requirements.md, checklists/testing.md) from an earlier spec-driven workflow that isn't being followed. Consolidate the valuable content into a single REQUIREMENTS.md (or similar) with sections: Constraints, Principles, Requirements, Key Decisions, Open Items. Also clean up scaffolding directories: .specify/ (templates, scripts, memory/constitution.md), .gemini/ (commands), _todo_resources/ (3 files: vscode-exts.md, fix-wsl-path.sh, win-term-profile.ps1 — migrate useful content first). The quickstart.md content should move to the root README.md (see sister issue).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:39Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:39Z","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: Consolidated 7 specs files into specs/REQUIREMENTS.md, deleted .specify/ and .gemini/ scaffolding

old_value: {"id":"dotfiles-qqw","title":"Investigate and automate maintenance tasks","description":"Review what maintenance the dotfiles repo needs and how much is already automated. Current automation: (1) package-updates.yml — weekly check for Homebrew outdated + Starship releases, creates GitHub issues. (2) ghostty-terminfo.yml — weekly regeneration of xterm-ghostty terminfo. (3) dependabot.yml — weekly GitHub Actions version bumps. (4) pre-commit — markdownlint-cli2. Gaps to investigate: (a) No automated chezmoi version tracking. (b) No Oh My Zsh plugin update detection. (c) No shell startup time regression monitoring. (d) No automated apt/winget package freshness checks (only Homebrew). (e) docs/MAINTENANCE.md describes monthly/quarterly/annual tasks but none are automated or tracked. (f) No Brewfile.lock or pinned versions for reproducibility. Propose which gaps are worth filling and how (GitHub Actions, chezmoi hooks, or manual checklist).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:52Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-02-28T16:48:52Z","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: Added chezmoi and OMZ plugin checks to weekly workflow, trimmed MAINTENANCE.md. PR #51.
README populated, docs trimmed (5→2 files), AGENTS.md trimmed (129→25 lines), CI references updated across CONTRIBUTING.md and TESTING.md

old_value: {"id":"dotfiles-n1e","title":"Bootstrap prerequisites in install.sh for fresh machines","description":"On a fresh macOS, git is just an Xcode CLT stub that triggers a dialog. install.sh needs to install Xcode CLT and Homebrew before chezmoi runs. On fresh Linux, git/curl may be missing too.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"github@pmgledhill.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:18Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:18Z"}

{"id":"dotfiles-6tx","title":"Fix missing git dependency on Ubuntu/Linux","description":"run_once_install-packages.sh uses git clone for OMZ plugins and nanorc, but git is not in the apt package list. On a fresh Ubuntu, git may not be present.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"github@pmgledhill.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:19Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:19Z"}

{"id":"dotfiles-lsp","title":"CI: strip pre-installed tools for realistic testing","description":"macOS/Ubuntu CI runners come with tools pre-installed (Homebrew, git, zsh) that mask bootstrap failures. Strip these after checkout to simulate a fresher state.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@pmgledhill.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:21Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-03-17T17:23:21Z"}

{"id":"dotfiles-qk7","title":"Replace Docker with Podman across all environments","description":"Switch container tooling from Docker to Podman. Remove Docker Desktop from Windows winget, add podman to work tier brew, add podman to Linux apt, add docker=podman alias, update Claude Code permissions and MCP config.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"github@pmgledhill.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T17:35:49Z","created_by":"Paul Gledhill","updated_at":"2026-03-17T17:35:49Z"}

new_value: Implemented: Podman replaces Docker across macOS (brew), Linux (apt), Windows (removed Docker Desktop). Shell aliases, Claude Code permissions, MCP setup all updated.
Evaluated and implemented: Podman chosen over Docker for all environments.
